name: Red Admiral
description: Migrates thousands of kilometres each year — some populations cross from North Africa to the Arctic Circle.

name: Common Lime Butterfly
description: One of India's most common garden butterflies; caterpillars feed exclusively on citrus leaves.

name: Common Mormon
description: Females come in three different forms — some mimicking toxic butterflies for protection.

name: Painted Lady
description: The most widely distributed butterfly in the world; found on every continent except Antarctica.

name: Common Jezebel
description: Brightly coloured wings warn predators that its body contains bitter toxins.

name: Common Rose
description: Body is bright crimson under the black wings — a warning to predators that it's poisonous.

name: Blue Mormon
description: One of India's largest butterflies and the state butterfly of Maharashtra.

name: Cabbage White
description: Caterpillars are notorious garden pests but adults are gentle flower visitors.

name: Plain Tiger
description: Featured in 3500-year-old Egyptian frescoes — one of the earliest butterflies ever depicted by humans.

name: Monarch
description: Travels up to 4800 kilometres on its annual migration from Canada to Mexico.
